message:
feat(fnp-python): passthrough wrappers for 10 trig + hyperbolic ufuncs

Continues the `k74v` ufunc wrapping series -- prior batch landed
log/exp/sqrt and friends in 2beace5 "passthrough wrappers for 10
core numpy ufuncs (k74v.1)". This commit adds the trig and
hyperbolic family so fnp-python exposes them as module attributes
available for future Rust-backed substitution without forcing
callers to reach into numpy directly.

New #[pyfunction]s (crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs):

Inverse trig:
- arcsin(x) -- real branch returns NaN outside [-1,1]; complex input
  returns complex.
- arccos(x) -- real branch returns NaN outside [-1,1]; complex input
  returns complex.
- arctan(x) -- real line domain, range [-pi/2, pi/2].
- arctan2(x1, x2) -- two-arg quadrant-aware variant in [-pi, pi];
  the only binary ufunc in this batch.

Inverse hyperbolic:
- arcsinh(x) -- defined everywhere on real and complex.
- arccosh(x) -- defined on x >= 1; real NaN below 1.
- arctanh(x) -- defined on (-1, 1); plus or minus 1 yields plus or
  minus inf with RuntimeWarning.

Hyperbolic:
- sinh(x), cosh(x), tanh(x) -- integer input promotes to float;
  complex input returns complex; saturation / overflow matches
  numpy.

Each wrapper calls `py.import("numpy")` and forwards via
`getattr(name)?.call1(...)` with the same Py<PyAny> in/out shape
the preceding ufunc batch uses, so adding these to the module is
pure registration work at the bottom of `fnp_python`.

Module registration (10 new `m.add_function(...)` entries in
`fnp_python`) and 10 matching `module.getattr(name).is_ok()` sanity
assertions in `core_ufunc_module_exports_all_core_unary_ufuncs_and_trig_family`.

New test `core_ufunc_batch2_trig_hyperbolic_match_numpy_across_scalar_array_and_round_trips`
(~140 LOC) exercises:
- arcsin/arccos on `[-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1]` vs numpy.allclose.
- arctan on the `[-100, -1, 0, 1, 100]` real line.
- arctan2 on 8-point quadrant grid (+/-1, +/-1 plus axes).
- arcsinh on the wide real range, arccosh on `[1, 1.5, 2, 10, 100]`,
  arctanh on `[-0.9, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 0.9]`.
- Hyperbolic sinh/cosh/tanh parity on a small real range.
- Round-trip identities: sin(arcsin(x))=x on [-1,1],
  tan(arctan(x))=x on real line, sinh(arcsinh(x))=x on real,
  tanh(arctanh(x))=x on (-1, 1) -- catches silent cross-family bugs.
- Canonical scalar checks: arccos(0)=pi/2, arctan2(1,0)=pi/2,
  arccosh(1)=0.
- Integer-input dtype promotion parity for sinh/cosh/tanh/arctan/
  arcsinh to lock down the integer-to-float dtype rule across the
  batch.

No behavioral change for callers already using numpy.<fn>; this is
a surface expansion on fnp-python that unblocks future Rust-owned
implementations (tracked on franken_numpy-hswd and the other hsw*
family port tasks).
